title: Mnemosyne
summary: In Greek mythology, Mnemosyne was one of the Titans, who are the children of Uranus (Sky) and Gaia (Earth). She was the goddess of memory and the mother of the nine Muses by Zeus. The name Mnemosyne is derived from the Greek word mnēmē, which means 'remembrance, memory'. According to Hesiod's Theogony, Zeus lay with Mnemosyne for nine consecutive nights, thereby begetting the nine Muses.
